events.title: US and Iran trade strikes across Middle East
events.desc: The United States and Iran traded air attacks for a second straight day, with President Donald Trump vowing further strikes if Tehran does not immediately agree to a peace deal. The escalation in hostilities began earlier this week with the downing of a U.S. Apache helicopter near the Strait of Hormuz, which sparked a series of tit-for-tat attacks across Iran and on U.S. bases around the region.

events.title: Iran announces closure of Strait of Hormuz
events.desc: Iran's top joint military command announced the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, including oil tankers and commercial ships, saying any vessel that attempts passage will be shot at. This follows a period of escalating strikes between Iran and the US.

events.title: US strikes Iran in response to downing of military helicopter
events.desc: President Donald Trump earlier accused Iran of shooting down the US helicopter over the Strait of Hormuz and vowed to respond. The US military conducted strikes on Iranian targets in response to the incident.

events.title: Three Indian sailors killed in US strike on oil tanker
events.desc: The US military attacked a Palau-flagged tanker after accusing it of not complying with directions, resulting in the deaths of three Indian sailors. This incident is part of the wider escalations in the region.

events.title: Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon kill 17
events.desc: Seventeen people were reported killed in a series of Israeli air strikes in southern Lebanon, with nine fatalities occurring in the town of Tayr Debba. This escalation follows warnings from Iran to cease attacks on its Lebanese ally.

events.title: UK, Australia, Canada launch $4m peace fund for Israel-Palestine conflict
events.desc: The United Kingdom, Australia, and Canada have launched a $4 million peace fund aimed at providing humanitarian support to Palestinians, cracking down on Israeli settlers, and supporting a Gaza peace plan. This initiative comes amidst ongoing conflict and tensions.

events.title: US and Iran exchange strikes around the region
events.desc: The US military struck Iranian drones and radar sites, while Tehran stated it targeted US bases in Kuwait and Bahrain. This exchange represents the latest in a series of retaliatory attacks following an earlier incident involving a US helicopter.

events.title: US releases video of warship firing missiles in strikes on Iran
events.desc: US Central Command released video footage showing what the military claims are 'self-defence' strikes on Iranian targets. This action is part of the ongoing exchange of hostilities between the two nations.

events.title: UN watchdog demands Iran provide information on nuclear stockpile
events.desc: The UN's nuclear watchdog has demanded Iran provide crucial information regarding its nuclear stockpile. Iran has criticized the resolution, labeling it 'politically motivated' and warning it could jeopardize ceasefire talks.

events.title: Bodies of Evidence: Sexual violence in Israeli prisons
events.desc: A report details claims of sexual violence against Palestinian prisoners in Israeli custody, particularly in the aftermath of October 7th. This follows years of reported mistreatment of detainees.

events.title: Iran warns Middle East truce 'practically meaningless' after US strikes
events.desc: Iran has warned that the fragile ceasefire in the Middle East war is now 'practically meaningless' following fresh strikes by the United States. Tehran has responded with attacks around the region, further undermining the truce.

events.title: The Pentagon’s AI Edge Is Being Distilled Away
events.desc: Adversaries can leverage publicly released frontier AI models that underpin Pentagon systems, effectively bypassing direct system breaches. This poses a significant risk as the Defense Department increasingly relies on AI, potentially eroding its technological advantage derived from AI model supremacy.

events.title: US and Iran exchange strikes for a second day, undermining shaky ceasefire
events.desc: The United States and Iran traded air attacks for a second straight day, with President Donald Trump vowing further strikes if Tehran does not immediately agree to a peace deal. The escalation in hostilities began earlier this week with the downing of a U.S. Apache helicopter near the Strait of Hormuz.

events.title: AirAsia X postpones Bahrain flight route due to regional conflict
events.desc: Malaysian low-cost airline AirAsia X has postponed the launch of its planned service via Bahrain until at least August due to the ongoing conflict in the Middle East. The route was initially set to commence in June.

events.title: Twenty-two countries warn Iran to stop attacks 'on our soil'
events.desc: Twenty-two countries, including the United States and European nations, have jointly warned Iran to cease attacks targeting individuals 'on our soil'. They condemned Iran's security services for allegedly using international and local criminal gangs for plots in Europe, North America, and Australia.
